topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box sat on my worn welcome mat, an unwelcome intrusion. It was addressed to 'Apartment 3B,' the unit I’d vacated three weeks prior. A faint, unfamiliar scent—something like old lavender and damp paper—wafted up as I nudged it with the toe of my slipper. My shoulders immediately slumped, a heavy, sinking feeling settling in my chest. I picked up the package, the tape feeling cold and impersonal under my fingertips. Who would even be sending this? A sudden wave of awkwardness washed over me. I shuffled toward the door, my pace slow, as if wading through thick water. I wondered what kind of situation this was, and if I should even bother opening it.

[story 2]

I found the parcel tucked behind the recycling bin, a stark, unexpected delivery. Seeing the address—the previous tenant’s details—made my stomach clench. A tightness gripped my throat, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I knelt down, my hands trembling slightly as I examined the label. It was clearly meant for someone else. A deep sigh escaped me, a sound heavy with unspoken regret. I stood up abruptly, knocking a stack of magazines askew. I retreated to the living room, staring blankly at the box, feeling a profound sense of obligation mixed with sheer discomfort about the whole messy arrangement.

[story 3]

The delivery arrived with a dull thud against the hallway wall. It was a box, clearly marked for the unit I had just emptied. My gaze drifted to it, and a knot formed tight in my chest, squeezing the air out of me. I felt a distinct urge to hide it, to somehow erase the evidence of my departure. My fingers hovered over the tape, hesitant to break the seal. A low murmur escaped my lips, barely audible, a sound of quiet acknowledgment of a mistake made. I backed away slowly, turning my back on the package, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole.
